mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate at 0.09% is near zero, indicating an accommodative monetary stance. A sustained period without rate increases signals continued monetary ease, which reduces real yields and causes nominal 10-year Treasury yields to decline as investors seek duration. This mechanism operates continuously in low-rate regimes.
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield rises by more than 15 basis points over 20 trading days despite FEDFUNDS remaining below 0.15%, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 12-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate at 7.6% signals labor market slack. When UNRATE exceeds 7.0%, it indicates persistent slack that constrains wage growth and inflation expectations, causing the Fed to maintain accommodative policy and Treasury yields to compress downward over the subsequent 20 trading days.
regime_hint: post-shock labor market recovery with slack
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield rises by more than 10 basis points in 20 trading days when UNRATE > 7.0%, or signal fails to predict yield direction in > 50% of triggered instances over 2-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Real sales (RSAFS at 422,794M) combined with housing starts (HOUST at 914K) measure aggregate demand momentum. When both series show month-over-month growth, it signals robust economic activity that raises inflation expectations and causes 10-year Treasury yields to rise as the market reprices growth and inflation risk over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both increase month-over-month
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield falls by more than 10 basis points when both RSAFS and HOUST increase month-over-month, or joint signal produces Rank IC < 0.04 over any 18-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO at 98.67) below the 2017=100 baseline indicates supply-side weakness. Sustained INDPRO below 99.0 signals constrained productive capacity, which raises inflation expectations and causes 10-year Treasury yields to rise as the market prices in persistent inflation risk over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield falls by more than 20 basis points over 60 trading days when INDPRO < 99.0, or signal produces negative Rank IC over any 12-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: CPI (CPIAUCSL at 231.831) combined with federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS at 0.09%) creates a large negative real rate of approximately -8% (nominal minus inflation). This extreme real rate accommodation signals monetary policy is highly stimulative, compressing term premiums and causing 10-year Treasury yields to decline over the next 5 trading days as the market reprices duration risk downward.
regime_hint: negative real rates with high inflation
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield rises by more than 8 basis points over 5 trading days when real rates are deeply negative, or interaction signal produces Rank IC < 0.06 over any 12-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS at 135,902K) is the primary labor market flow indicator. When PAYEMS declines month-over-month by more than 100K, it signals deteriorating labor demand that reduces inflation expectations and causes 10-year Treasury yields to fall as the market reprices growth and inflation risk downward over the next 20 trading days.
regime_hint: labor market deterioration or recession risk
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield rises by more than 12 basis points over 20 trading days following a PAYEMS decline > 100K, or signal produces Rank IC < 0.05 over any 18-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP (GDPC1 at 13,725.7B) growth trajectory combined with real sales momentum (RSAFS) determines aggregate demand strength. When both series show positive quarter-over-quarter growth, it signals sustained economic expansion that raises inflation expectations and causes 10-year Treasury yields to rise over the next 60 trading days as the market reprices terminal rate expectations.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 and RSAFS both show positive growth
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield falls by more than 25 basis points over 60 trading days when both GDPC1 and RSAFS show positive growth, or interaction signal produces Rank IC < 0.04 over any 24-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) combined with payroll employment (PAYEMS) measures supply-side capacity and labor utilization. When INDPRO rises above 99.5 and PAYEMS increases month-over-month, it signals strong productive capacity utilization that raises inflation expectations and causes 10-year Treasury yields to rise over the next 20 trading days as the market reprices inflation risk.
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield falls by more than 12 basis points over 20 trading days when both INDPRO > 99.5 and PAYEMS increase, or interaction signal produces Rank IC < 0.05 over any 18-month rolling window.
